commit.message:
fix: actualizar Next.js 15.1.3 → 15.5.13 (CVE-2025-66478)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add EVM/Scheduler/Contracts validators + localnet/doctor CLI tests + fix Unicode panic

New validators:
- validate_evm_address(): 0x-prefixed 20-byte hex with helpful errors
- validate_hex_data(): general hex string validator (odd-length, bad chars)
- validate_pallet_call(): pallet/call name validation (PascalCase/snake_case)
- validate_schedule_id(): task ID validation (non-empty, ≤32 bytes)

Integrated into handlers:
- EVM: call validates source/target/input/value/fee; withdraw validates address
- Scheduler: schedule/schedule-named validates pallet, call, and task ID
- Preimage: note validates pallet/call; unnote validates hash hex
- Contracts: instantiate validates code-hash/data/salt hex; call validates
  contract SS58 + data hex; remove-code validates code-hash hex

Bug fix:
- validate_pallet_call() panicked on Unicode input >128 bytes due to byte
  slicing (same class as previous short_ss58/truncate/validate_ss58 fixes)
  Fixed with chars().take(32).collect()

104 new tests (1,450 total, was 1,346):
- 20 validate_evm_address tests (valid/invalid/empty/short/long/odd/unicode)
- 11 validate_hex_data tests (empty/odd/invalid/0x-only/0X prefix)
- 16 validate_pallet_call tests (PascalCase/snake_case/empty/dash/dot/unicode/long)
- 7 validate_schedule_id tests (empty/long/special chars)
- 24 localnet CLI tests (start/stop/status/reset/logs/scaffold + boundary)
- 4 doctor CLI tests (plain/json/network/wallet)
- 12 EVM boundary tests (max gas/overflow/ABI input/value/missing args)
- 6 scheduler boundary tests (repeat params/max when/overflow)
- 8 contracts boundary tests (storage limit/full args/max gas)
- 12 proptest fuzz tests (no-panic + roundtrip for all 4 new validators)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Design: upstream DVaaS integration (#340)

## Summary

Design proposal for making 4ward a drop-in replacement for BMv2 in
DVaaS,
upstreamed into sonic-pins as a `bazel_dep`.

- **Direct integration, not a backend.** Since 4ward has a hermetic
build,
we skip the `DataplaneValidationBackend` abstraction and integrate
output
  prediction + traces directly into `dataplane_validation.cc`.
- **Opt-in and backward compatible.** A new `fourward_config` field on
  `P4Specification` — when present, DVaaS uses 4ward; when absent, BMv2
  works unchanged.
- **Three deliverables:** p4c backend plugin (build-time compilation),
  P4Runtime server binary (test-time subprocess), and proto definitions.
- **FourwardMirrorTestbed** stays as a long-term development vehicle.

This is a design doc for discussion — no code changes.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: Map node click not working - P0.3 bug fix

- Set mouse_filter = IGNORE on MainMenu when hidden to prevent it from blocking map input
- Added explicit mouse_filter = STOP (0) on MapNode to ensure it receives mouse events
- Updated IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md to mark P0.3 as complete

The issue was that the MainMenu was covering the map even when hidden,
preventing map node clicks from registering. By setting mouse_filter to
IGNORE, the hidden menu no longer intercepts mouse input.

commit.message:
[gpo-frontend] INTERACTION BUG FIX: scroll hijack, repeated toasts, quick start copy, feedback UX

BUG 1 — REPEATED "TASK COMPLETED" TOAST + SCROLL HIJACK:

Root causes found and fixed:
a) Intake detail poll (line 1745): on task completion, called showIntakeDetail()
   which triggers scrollIntoView() → scroll jump to top of panel
   FIX: removed showIntakeDetail() re-call on completion. Only re-render in place.

b) Intake detail poll (line 1747): showToast fires on every poll cycle where
   status is done/failed, not just the first time
   FIX: added wasComplete guard — toast only fires on status TRANSITION to done/failed

c) SSE task handler (line 99): fires SECOND completion toast for the same task
   FIX: suppress SSE toast when intake detail panel is active (poll handles it)

d) showIntakeDetail (line 1772): scrollIntoView fires every time including re-fetches
   FIX: only scroll on FIRST open (wasHidden check), not on poll re-fetch

e) SSE debounced loadIntakeTasks (line 82): re-renders task list every 3s while
   intake tab is active, causing layout shift during reading
   FIX: skip list refresh when detail panel is open

BUG 2 — RESULT REVIEW STABILITY:
- renderIntakeDetail now updates in-place without scroll
- Poll stops silently when task is already complete (no toast, no re-render)
- Detail panel re-fetch doesn't trigger scroll

BUG 3 — QUICK START COPY CLEANUP:
- Removed all Rahul-specific language ("data engineer", "Austin TX", "180k+")
- Reduced from 16 templates to 11 focused ones
- Cleaned awkward phrasing in prompts
- Migrated legacy domain IDs: careeregine→career, wealthresearch→finance,
  personalops→ops, newsroom→news
- Removed "Job Search" quick prompt from Ask AI (too specific)

BUG 4 — FEEDBACK UX:
- Changed "Rate output:" → "Was this helpful?" (clearer context)
- Changed "Good / Needs Work / Bad" → "✓ Yes / Could be better / ✗ No"
- Added margin for visual separation from action buttons

Files changed:
- app.js: poll logic, SSE toast guard, scroll guard, feedback copy
- operator.js: all 11 templates rewritten
- index.html: Ask AI quick prompts cleaned

Backend behavior preserved: YES

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: rename score, fix mobile UX, Shorts filter, show-more bug

Dashboard:
- Rename "Gravity Score" -> "Requester Score" everywhere (JSON-LD,
  cards, detail pane, about popover, about section, schema markup)
- Fix "Show more" in source thread cards: button click was also
  triggering the thread card link (navigating away). toggleExpand()
  now calls event.stopPropagation() + preventDefault().
- Fix contact email: about-section-email is declared after the
  <script> block so the old IIFE couldn't find it. Moved wiring
  into DOMContentLoaded via _wireContactLinks().
- Mobile overhaul:
  * Header: 54px, hide tagline + status pill on <=640px
  * Hero: compact padding, stacked layout, smaller h1
  * Cards: tighter padding, slightly smaller font
  * Source filter pills: horizontal scroll (no wrap) on mobile
  * Sidebar becomes a bottom sheet on mobile: fixed position,
    slides up from bottom with 82vh max-height, drag handle,
    accent-color top border, backdrop blur overlay
  * Platform/category widgets hidden in sheet (only detail shown)
  * X close button in detail widget header (mobile-only)
  * closeMobileDetail() deselects card + dismisses sheet
  * _openMobileSheet() called from selectRequest() when width<=640

Scraper (requester_phase1.py):
- YouTube: add videoDuration=medium to search API call, filtering
  out Shorts (< 60s) which the API returns alongside normal videos.
  "medium" = 4-20 min videos, covers typical complaint/review videos.

fix(epg): enable WorkManager scheduling on TV to survive Doze mode

EPG auto-refresh at 2am/3am consistently failed on Shield TV with DNS
errors because Doze mode blocks network access. The coroutine-based
scheduler fires on time but has no Doze awareness. Remove the
isFixedDevice() guard so TV devices also use WorkManager, which defers
work to Doze maintenance windows when network is available.

Also use formatTimestamp for EPG status display on both mobile and TV.

commit.message:
fix: CRDT repaired-entry no-op resolve + worker_channel stale-session poisoning

Bug fixes from deep agent code review:

1. obligation/crdt.rs: record_resolve() now early-returns for repaired
   (tombstoned) entries. Previously it incremented resolve_counts and
   joined witnesses only to have normalize_repair_tombstone() immediately
   discard all the work. This wasted computation and masked the fact that
   repaired entries are terminal — resolving them should be a no-op,
   matching the existing record_acquire() guard for terminal entries.

2. net/worker_channel.rs: BootstrapFailed handler now clears
   active_worker_id instead of setting it to the failed worker's ID.
   Previously, a failed worker whose bootstrap was rejected could still
   send follow-up messages (Diagnostic, ShutdownCompleted) that passed
   the session check, because its worker_id matched the stored
   active_worker_id. A ShutdownCompleted from the dead worker would
   clear the coordinator's state, potentially interfering with a
   replacement worker's bootstrap sequence.

Also includes audit_index entries for deep security audits (batch 387):
h1/server+codec, postgres, tls/connector, h2/connection, load_balance,
scheduler/stealing+local_queue, mpsc, obligation/ledger.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: auth refactor, swipe gallery, video seekbar, media save, SDK update

Auth (Privy as single source of truth):
- useAuth: thin wrapper over usePrivy(), no module-level state
- isAuthenticated derived from !!user (Expo SDK has no isAuthenticated)
- profileStore: removed address/userId (Privy manages auth identity)
- RegistrationScreen: login → waitForWallet → syncUser → navigate
  with address/userId passed via navigation params (no async race)
- PublishingScreen: receives address from params directly
- PublishedGalleryScreen: fetches content based on Privy auth state
- SettingsScreen: profile section hidden when logged out, login button
  when logged out, logout with spinner feedback

Camera gallery (SwipeGalleryView):
- Full-screen swipe gallery for camera thumbnail access
- FlatList horizontal paging with virtualization (windowSize=3)
- Image pinch-to-zoom via @likashefqet/react-native-image-zoom
- Video playback with tap-to-play, auto-pause on swipe away
- LINE-style selection indicator (numbered circle)
- Shared VideoSeekBar component (Gesture Handler based)

VideoSeekBar (shared component):
- Used in both SwipeGalleryView and EditScreen
- Pan + tap gestures via react-native-gesture-handler
- Animated progress fill + thumb via react-native-reanimated
- runOnJS for thread-safe callbacks

Media save:
- saveToRootLensAlbum: saves to DCIM/RootLens/ album
- Filename format: RL_YYYYMMDD_HHmmss_NNN.ext (no c2pa-signed prefix)

SDK & naming:
- @title-protocol/sdk updated to 0.1.4
- phash-v1 → image-phash (processorId, extension_id, WASM filename)

commit.message:
[gpo-frontend] Batch 10: Final cleanup — operator.js migrated + dead CSS removed + sidebar polished

OPERATOR.JS SECONDARY SURFACES MIGRATED:

1. renderMissionTemplates():
   .template-card → .surface with design token padding
   .tc-domain → .badge.badge-neutral
   .tc-title/.tc-desc → styled divs with font-weight/size tokens
   .tc-run-btn → .btn.btn-ghost.btn-sm

2. Mission snapshot grid (renderMissionSnapshot):
   .mission-card-enhanced → .surface
   .mce-header/name → flex with font-weight
   .mce-objective → styled text
   .mce-meta/blocker/progress/next → styled 9px text
   .mission-badge → .badge with status variants

3. Chief of Staff brief (loadChiefOfStaffBrief):
   .cos-action → .surface with design token padding
   .cos-priority → .badge with priority variants (danger/warning/neutral)
   .cos-action-title → styled span
   .cos-badge → .badge.badge-warning/danger
   .cos-action-btn → .btn.btn-ghost.btn-sm
   .cos-action-why → styled text
   .cos-action-meta → .badge.badge-neutral + styled spans

DEAD CSS REMOVED (second pass):
   10 rule blocks removed from style.css + operator.css
   Classes: .template-card (13 rules), .tc-*, .mission-card-enhanced, .mce-*,
            .cos-badge, .cos-approval, .cos-blocked, .cos-action-meta, .cos-scope/alignment/cap
   Total CSS cleanup across Batch 9+10: 81 rule blocks (221+ lines)

SIDEBAR POLISHED:
   .nav-role-label: removed !important overrides, switched to design tokens
   Cleaner font-weight (700→600), letter-spacing (1.2→1), margin/padding tokens

CSS SIZE PROGRESSION:
   Start of redesign: 2351 lines
   After Batch 9: 2140 lines
   After Batch 10: 2139 lines (+ operator.css cleanup)

VISUAL DIFF SUMMARY:
Templates — Before: custom .template-card with .tc-* sub-classes
Templates — After: .surface cards with .badge for domain, .btn for run action

Mission snapshots — Before: .mission-card-enhanced with .mce-* sub-classes
Mission snapshots — After: .surface cards with .badge for status, styled text

Chief of Staff — Before: .cos-action with .cos-* badge/meta classes
Chief of Staff — After: .surface cards with .badge for priority/status, .btn for actions

Backend behavior preserved: YES

FRONTEND COHERENCE ASSESSMENT:
The frontend is now coherent enough to exit redesign mode.
Every visible operational surface uses the new design system:
- Dashboard, Tasks, Intake, Approvals, Engines, Memory, Providers, Costs, Activity, Operations, Settings, Ask AI
- Intake detail panel, task modal, templates, mission snapshots, Chief of Staff brief
- All use: .surface, .btn, .badge, .section-hdr, .metric-tile, .empty-state, .feedback-bar

Remaining weak areas (polish-only, not redesign):
- Some operator.js health/engine detail renders still have old patterns
- Full mobile testing needed beyond breakpoint rules
- Sidebar hover expand animation could be smoother

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(cli): collect types from specifier-level type-only re-exports (#230)

## Why

`export { type Foo } from "..."` (specifier-level type-only re-export)
was silently ignored during schema generation. Both the
type-name-collector (Phase 1) and type-extractor (Phase 2) only checked
`node.isTypeOnly` on the `ExportDeclaration` node, missing the case
where individual export specifiers have `isTypeOnly = true`.

This meant types re-exported with the per-specifier syntax were not
collected, resulting in missing type definitions in the generated
GraphQL schema.

## Summary

- Add handling for specifier-level type-only exports (`export { type Foo
} from "..."`) in both `type-name-collector.ts` and `type-extractor.ts`
- Add `export-declaration-specifier-type` golden test case to verify the
fix

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

1. Removed hint prepend from handleMessageRelay() — receive, forward, return.
2. Deleted TOOL_FAMILY_BLOCKS + can_use_tool blocking — confirmed theater,
   platform plugins bypass Bridge control requests entirely.
3. Removed assembleToolHintSlot import from server.ts.

tool-hint-slot.ts NOT deleted — still called by assembler.ts (Chrome Extension
pipeline via processEnvelope). Only the Telegram relay path is cleaned.

Diagnostic removed (DIAG logs).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add framework coexistence: migrate existing CLAUDE.md to rules/ (v2.3.0)

Frameworks like AIDLC use CLAUDE.md as their steering mechanism, conflicting
with bp-setup's template. New option (c) in Phase 1 migrates existing CLAUDE.md
to .claude/rules/<user-named>.md, then deploys the best practices template.
Claude loads both files every session -- no content lost, no conflicts.

Safeguards from simulation: token budget warning (>5k tokens), solo mode
routing (always project-level), conflict warning, re-run guard (hides
option c when CLAUDE.md is already our template).

commit.message:
fix: add "default" export condition to all publishConfig exports

Node's CJS resolver (require()) needs "default" or "require" conditions
in the exports map. Without it, ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED is thrown
when nx's node executor loads packages via require().

Adds "default" pointing to the same file as "import" for all 16 packages
that have publishConfig.exports.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix ruling lines on all cells, remove income tax from equity, fix duplicate entity name, page breaks

Fix 1 — Ruling lines applied to ALL cells in summary rows:
Previously borders only applied to amount cells [2,3]. Now applies
single top + single bottom to every cell on subtotal rows, and
single top + double bottom (sz=12) on every cell on grand total rows.
Also bold all text in summary rows. Applied in both template generator
(_apply_subtotal_borders, _apply_grand_total_borders now iterate all
cells) and post-processing. Added borders to Summary P&L rows 3 and 5.

Fix 2 — Cover page: already at position 0 in DOCUMENT_TYPE_ORDER,
builder exists. Will render after generate_fs_templates --force.

Fix 3 — Duplicate entity name: _add_watermark_header() was adding
{{ entity_name }} to the Word section header AND each builder added
it to the document body, causing duplication on the first page.
Removed entity name from the header — it now only contains the
DRAFT watermark text. Entity name appears once in the body only.

Fix 4 — Sign convention: _format_lines(credit_normal=True) correctly
negates credit balances for liabilities and equity. Accounts showing
negative have genuine debit balances (refunds/overpayments). No code
change needed — behaviour is correct.

Fix 5 — Income tax removed from equity: _get_tb_sections() mapped
ALL codes 4000-4999 to equity, but codes 4100-4149 are income tax
expense (IS-TAX) — a P&L item already reflected in net profit and
in current liabilities as "Taxation". Now excludes income tax codes
and name-matched accounts from the equity section.

Fix 6 — Page break improvements:
- Compilation Report: added keep_with_next to final body paragraphs
  and signature block so they stay together.
- Balance Sheet: removed empty spacer paragraph before equity section
  that was forcing an unnecessary page break.
- Notes: note headings already have keep_with_next from prior session.

Post-deploy: run python3 manage.py generate_fs_templates --force

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: proactive map visualization + travel planning skill (#51)

* feat: proactive map visualization + travel planning best practices

Problems fixed:
1. AI never calls static_map unless explicitly asked → added PROACTIVELY
   hint to static_map description
2. Trip plans cluster in one area → added district-based exploration
   hints to explore_area and plan_route descriptions
3. No travel planning methodology → new references/travel-planning.md
   with anti-patterns, time budgets, correct flow

Changes:
- static_map: "PROACTIVELY call after explore_area, plan_route, etc."
- explore_area: "call multiple times with district names, not city name"
- plan_route: "geographic arc per day, call static_map after"
- New: references/travel-planning.md (complete methodology)
- Recipe 1 rewritten: district-first, always-visualize approach
- SKILL.md: added travel-planning.md to reference table

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: tool-driven travel planning — search_places as anchor discovery

Replaces AI-knowledge-dependent district approach with tool-driven flow:
1. search_places("top attractions in {city}") → naturally diverse anchors
2. Cluster anchors by proximity → assign to days as geographic arcs
3. explore_area around each anchor → find supporting restaurants/cafes
4. plan_route per day → static_map per day

Verified: search_places("top attractions in Kyoto") returns points spanning
8km×10.7km across Fushimi/Higashiyama/Kinkaku-ji/Arashiyama — natural
geographic diversity without needing pre-knowledge of city districts.

Also: static_map description now says PROACTIVELY call after other tools.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add maps_search_along_route + travel planning methodology overhaul

Search Along Route (new tool):
- Uses Google Places searchText API with searchAlongRouteParameters
- Internally: directions(A→B) → get polyline → searchText along polyline
- Results ranked by minimal detour time, not proximity
- Tested: Fushimi Inari→Kiyomizu-dera finds 5 along-route restaurants

Tool description improvements:
- static_map: PROACTIVELY call after search/route tools
- explore_area: use district names not city name
- plan_route: geographic arc per day

Travel planning methodology (references/travel-planning.md):
- Tool-driven geographic spread via search_places anchors
- 6-layer decision model from research
- Anti-pattern table + time budget guidelines
- Search Along Route as core filling mechanism

All 9 files synced (16 → 17 tools). 132 smoke test assertions, 0 failures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: complete project knowledge skill package

6 reference files forming a comprehensive knowledge package:

New files:
- architecture.md — system architecture, transport modes, tool registration, code map
- google-maps-api-guide.md — API endpoints, pricing, coverage, common gotchas
- geo-domain-knowledge.md — GIS fundamentals, coordinates, spatial search, Japan context
- decisions.md — 10 ADRs with context and rationale

Updated files:
- travel-planning.md — complete 6-layer model with Search Along Route
- tools-api.md — Recipe 1 rewritten with search_along_route, added tool docs
- SKILL.md — expanded reference table (6 files, 3 categories)

Reading this skill package gives full capability to maintain, develop,
and make informed decisions about this project.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: split skills into agent skill + project-docs skill

Agent Skill (skills/google-maps/) — how to USE the tools:
  - SKILL.md, tools-api.md, travel-planning.md

Project Docs Skill (skills/project-docs/) — how to DEVELOP/MAINTAIN:
  - SKILL.md, architecture.md, google-maps-api-guide.md,
    geo-domain-knowledge.md, decisions.md (10 ADRs)

Separation ensures the agent skill stays focused on tool usage
(published to skill registries) while project knowledge lives
in its own skill for developer onboarding.

The scrollytelling scripts were computing zone demographics independently
via BG-fragment interpolation, producing values that diverged from the
interactive map's JS histogram aggregation. Now:

- school_socioeconomic_analysis.py exports census_dot_zone_demographics.csv
  with per-zone demographics computed from the same dot/block data embedded
  in the interactive map (matching JS updateHistograms exactly)
- median_hh_income is the true population-weighted median (matching the
  histogram red line), not the mean of block-group medians
- Both story scripts read this CSV for zone and drive demographics,
  merging supplemental columns (AH, MLS, dev) from the existing CSV

commit.message:
fix: overhaul dedup — 190 demands reduced to 135 (55 merged)

Scraper changes (requester_phase1.py):
- FIX ORDERING BUG: subject normalization now runs BEFORE all three
  dedup passes. Previously it ran after Pass 1+2, so "Delta App" and
  "Delta Airlines" had different subjects when fuzzy dedup compared
  them — causing missed merges. Now both normalize to "Delta" first.
- Fuzzy dedup: remove category constraint (Claude labels inconsistently
  across batches). Two demands can merge on subject + keyword overlap
  alone, regardless of category.
- Fuzzy threshold: 45% -> 35% (keyword overlap is inherently limited
  for semantically equivalent but lexically different phrases).
- LLM dedup prompt: removed "be conservative" instruction; replaced
  with explicit merge/keep-separate rules and "when in doubt, MERGE".
  Previous prompt kept 4 variants of "restore open seating" as separate
  demands; new prompt correctly consolidates them.

Data (requester_results.json):
- Applied improved LLM dedup to current results immediately.
- Notable merges: 4 Southwest "restore open seating" variants -> 1,
  2 Southwest "bag fee" variants -> 1, 5 United app crash variants -> 1,
  5+ Delta login/crash variants, plus merges for Netflix/Alaska/
  American/Amazon/eBay/DoorDash demands.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
[gpo-frontend] Batch 9: Intake detail + task modal + dead CSS cleanup + mobile hardening

INTAKE DETAIL PANEL (renderIntakeDetail) REDESIGNED:
- .delib-panel → .surface with design token padding
- .delib-section/.delib-section-title/.delib-section-body → styled divs with uppercase labels
- .intake-hero-btn.btn-deliberate → .btn.btn-primary.btn-lg (full-width)
- .intake-hero-btn.btn-approve-exec → .btn.btn-success.btn-lg (full-width)
- .cos-action-btn → .btn.btn-secondary/.btn-ghost.btn-sm
- .risk-badge → .badge.badge-danger/warning/success
- .domain-tag + .intake-status-badge → .badge system
- Feedback buttons → .btn.btn-success/danger.btn-sm inside .feedback-bar
- Deliberation section → .surface-inset with structured layout
- Loading state → .loading-state
- Error state → .empty-state with red icon

TASK MODAL (showTask) REDESIGNED:
- Plain text dump → structured HTML with .badge system
- Error display → styled pre with red-soft background
- Output → styled pre with bg-inset, max-height scroll
- Files written → mono-font list with design tokens
- Status/type/model → .badge chips

DEAD CSS CLEANUP:
- 71 rule blocks removed (211 lines)
- style.css: 2351 → 2140 lines
- Removed classes: .send-btn, .refresh-btn, .page-header, .header-row, .header-actions,
  .ctrl-btn, .board-btn, .ctrl-icon, .ctrl-desc, .cost-card,
  .channel-selector/panel/input-area/textarea/input-bar/select/body/section,
  .ch-dot, .settings-select/input/toggle, .model-row/name/detail,
  .approval-card, .filter-btn, .active-filter,
  .intake-card-header/title, .running-task-hero/label,
  .mission-name, .delib-panel/section-title/section-body

MOBILE RESPONSIVENESS:
- Added responsive grid collapse for inline grid styles at 768px
- 2-column grids → 1-column on tablet
- 3/4-column grids → 2-column on tablet

VISUAL DIFF SUMMARY:
Before: Intake detail was a monolithic .delib-panel with custom section styling, old button classes, old badge classes
After: .surface container with .badge system, .btn system, .surface-inset for deliberation, .feedback-bar for ratings

Before: Task modal was plain text in a pre element
After: Structured HTML with status badges, styled error/output sections, design token spacing

Before: 2351 lines of CSS with 86+ dead rules
After: 2146 lines, dead rules removed, cleaner

Backend behavior preserved: YES

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

v0.2.0: self-contained, vendored math, lake interpolation, solar7 colormaps

Major changes since v0.1.0:
- Fully self-contained: all math vendored, no external repo dependencies
- Pure Rust HDF5 reader is now the default (zero system library deps)
- Lake interpolation for 2m fields (lake_interp parameter)
- Solarpower07 colormaps and 41 product presets (style="solar7")
- Fixed STP: correct parcel types + CIN term for effective layer
- Pressure-based lapse rate bounds (bottom_p/top_p)
- 83 total variables, all verified on real WRF output

commit.message:
Fix critical security vulnerabilities and reliability issues

Security fixes:
- Remove hardcoded session secret fallback (require SESSION_SECRET env var)
- Restrict /api/auth/register to authenticated users only
- Disable debug endpoints and Playwright workshop in production
- Remove debug agent logging (127.0.0.1:7661 data leak)
- Remove all token prefix logging (HubSpot access token leak)
- Fix hardcoded encryption salt with random per-credential salt
- Remove hardcoded personal email fallbacks across all files

Reliability fixes:
- Add mutex to HubSpot token refresh (prevent race condition on single-use tokens)
- Fix expired HubSpot token fallthrough (throw instead of returning stale token)
- Remove duplicate Procore token refresh in hubspot-procore-sync.ts (use canonical mutex-protected version)
- Add mutex to proactive HubSpot token refresh path
